perturbation.original_text: The following information is excluded from being considered Confidential Information:(i) Information which, at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain, or information whichsubsequently becomes part of the public domain, by means other than breach of this Agreement.(ii) Information which the receiving Party obtains from a third party having a lawful right to disclosesaid information
perturbation.changed_text: The following information is excluded from being considered Confidential Information:(i) Information which, at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain, or information whichsubsequently becomes part of the public domain, by means other than breach of this Agreement.(ii) Information which the receiving Party obtains from an external source having a lawful right to disclosesaid information
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'third party' to 'external source' introduces ambiguity. A third party is generally understood in legal terms. An 'external source' is not defined. This contradiction arises in determining what sources of information are exempt. A third party is a legal term, while the other is not, hence becoming contradictory.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The following information is excluded from being considered Confidential Information:(i) Information which, at the time of disclosure, is in the public domain, or information whichsubsequently becomes part of the public domain, by means other than breach of this Agreement.(ii) Information which the receiving Party obtains from an external source having a lawful right to disclosesaid information
perturbation.justification: The change from 'third party' to 'external source' introduces ambiguity and misalignment of terminology. The original text uses 'third party,' a term with established legal meaning. The changed text replaces it with 'external source,' an undefined term. Since the definition of 'external source' is not provided in the document, it is unclear if it includes or excludes entities that would have been considered 'third parties,' hence creating a contradiction in how confidential information is treated. The Contradicted Text is the same as the Changed Text, so it is repeated in the document, causing confusion to what is actually considered a Third Party versus an External Source. Therefore, this is a Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will not, and will direct their respective representatives and associates not to, disclose toany other person that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction. The term "person" used in this Agreement shall be broadlyinterpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, corporation, company, group partnership orother entity.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will not, and will direct their respective representatives and associates not to, disclose toany other entity that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction. The term "person" used in this Agreement shall be broadlyinterpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, corporation, company, group partnership orother entity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'person' to 'entity' creates contradiction because later in the same section, 'person' is defined to include individuals. By using 'entity' at the start, and then broadly defining 'person' to include 'individual' we create a contradiction, on whether an individual can now be disclosed to. This restricts the scope of allowed disclosures and the contract is now contradicting itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties will not, and will direct their respective representatives and associates not to, disclose toany other entity that the Confidential Information has been made available to them, that discussions ornegotiations are taking place concerning a potential Transaction or any of the terms, conditions or otherfacts with respect to any such Transaction. The term "person" used in this Agreement shall be broadlyinterpreted to include, without limitation, any individual, corporation, company, group partnership orother entity.
perturbation.justification: The original text uses 'person' and then defines 'person' broadly. The changed text replaces 'person' with 'entity'. However, the subsequent definition of 'person' remains, including 'individual'. This creates a contradiction because the changed text prohibits disclosure to any 'entity', while the definition of 'person' (which includes 'individual') is still present. It now becomes unclear whether disclosure to an individual is permitted or not, since an individual is a 'person' but not necessarily an 'entity' which causes in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the ConfidentialInformation confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.changed_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the Proprietary Information confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Information' at the start, but not elsewhere, introduces confusion. The obligation is to protect 'Proprietary Information', but the action of disclosing is still forbidden for 'Confidential Information', making it unclear what information the parties are obligated to protect and what information they are forbidden to disclose. This contradicts the purpose of the entire NDA.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Parties will (and shall ensure that their respective Representatives will) keep the Proprietary Information confidential and will not, without the prior written consent of the other Party, disclose anyConfidential Information in any manner whatsoever, in whole or in part, and will not use ConfidentialInformation, directly or indirectly, for other than evaluating a possible Transaction. The Parties shall Reciprocal Confidential Non-Disclosure Agreement 3.12 of 3make all reasonable, necessary or appropriate efforts to safeguard the Confidential Information fromdisclosure to anyone other than as permitted hereby.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information' consistently. The changed text replaces the first instance of 'Confidential Information' with 'Proprietary Information', while still referring to 'Confidential Information' in subsequent clauses. This creates a direct conflict and ambiguity, contradicting the original intent. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, indicating that the modified text now directly conflicts with itself due to the change. The explanation provided clearly points out that it is unclear which information parties are obligated to protect, because it uses different terms. This aligns with the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology' leading to in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Party would not have an adequate remedy at lawand would be irreparably harmed if any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed inaccordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached. Accordingly, the Parties agree thateither Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specificallyenforce the terms and provisions hereof in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at lawor in equity.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Entity would not have an adequate remedy at lawand would be irreparably harmed if any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed inaccordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached. Accordingly, the Parties agree thateither Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specificallyenforce the terms and provisions hereof in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at lawor in equity.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Party' to 'Entity' at the beginning, when previously defined as 'Parties', introduces contradiction. Now the agreement acknowledges harm to the 'Entity', but injunctive relief is only available to a 'Party'. It is now not entirely clear whether an Entity can claim the same injunctive relief, creating a in-text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each Party acknowledges and agrees that the other Entity would not have an adequate remedy at lawand would be irreparably harmed if any of the provisions of this Agreement were not performed inaccordance with their specific terms or were otherwise breached. Accordingly, the Parties agree thateither Party shall be entitled to injunctive relief to prevent breaches of this Agreement and to specificallyenforce the terms and provisions hereof in addition to any other remedy to which it may be entitled at lawor in equity.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Party' to 'Entity' in the modified text introduces a contradiction because the original text defines specific rights and obligations for 'Parties'. The contradicted text maintains the 'Entity' reference alongside 'Party' granting injunctive relief to 'Parties' but implying a distinction between the two. The explanation states that it is now unclear whether an 'Entity' can claim the same injunctive relief, thus creating a contradiction as it is uncertain if the term 'Entity' has the same rights as 'Party' elsewhere in the contract which fulfills the definition of Misaligned Terminology.
